ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Rillaboom,' National Pokedex no. 812, large green gorilla with tree-stump drum — matches Rillaboom canonical design exactly. PokemonTCG.io confirms card name directly.

trivia_bullets.text: Rillaboom is National Pokedex no. 812, the final Stage 2 evolution of Grookey — the Galar Grass starter introduced in Pokemon Sword and Shield (2019). The Grookey line evolves: Grookey (level 16) to Thwackey, then to Rillaboom (level 35). [PokemonTCG.io: swsh6-18; WebSearch: Bulbapedia snippet]

Rillaboom's design references Taiko drumming and the chest-beating display gorillas use to assert social dominance. In a Rillaboom troop, the individual with the best drumming technique becomes leader — challenges for leadership are settled through drum battles. Galarian folklore holds that while Rillaboom drums, the rules of nature are suspended and predator and prey dance together. [WebSearch: Bulbapedia snippet / pokemongohub.net snippet]
Rillaboom's hair design is based on the Green Man, a pagan symbol found across Great Britain representing growth and renewal from Celtic folklore. [WebSearch: pokemongohub.net snippet]
'The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.' [PokemonTCG.io: swsh6-18]

This Chilling Reign print is a Rapid Strike subtype, matching the Rapid Strike mechanic introduced in Battle Styles. Its Raging Repeated Strike attack scales with Energy discarded — a discard-loop payoff that pairs with Rapid Strike Energy. [PokemonTCG.io: swsh6-18]
